title: Highway Code + Case Law Fault Reference Tool with Insurer-Style Fault Matrix

requirements.text: Create a dedicated standalone page or section for the Highway Code + case law fault reference tool, accessible from the main navigation. This page should allow users to describe or select their accident scenario (e.g. rear-end, red light, lane change, junction) and receive a structured fault assessment based on UK Highway Code rules and established case law precedents.

quotes: Highway Code + case law fault reference tool with the insurer-style fault matrix

acceptanceCriteria: A dedicated route or prominent section exists for the fault reference tool
Users can select or describe a common accident scenario type
The tool displays relevant Highway Code rules for the selected scenario
The tool displays relevant Road Traffic Act 1988 sections where applicable
The tool displays relevant UK case law precedents for the selected scenario

requirements.text: Display an insurer-style fault matrix for the selected accident scenario, showing Party A and Party B fault percentages alongside a plain-English rationale. The matrix should highlight the active scenario row or cell based on the user's selection. Expand the existing faultMatrix.ts static data if needed to cover all common scenario types (rear-end, red light, lane change, turning, junction, roundabout, reversing).
acceptanceCriteria: The fault matrix renders with Party A and Party B fault percentage columns
A rationale string is shown for the active scenario
The active scenario row or cell is visually highlighted
At least 7 common scenario types are covered in the data (rear-end, red light, lane change, turning, junction, roundabout, reversing)
Fault percentages are displayed as visual bars or badges alongside numeric values

requirements.text: Provide a plain-English scenario selector UI that lets users choose their accident type from a list of common scenarios (with icons or short descriptions). On selection, the fault matrix, Highway Code rules, and case law references all update to reflect the chosen scenario. The interface must be usable without any prior legal knowledge.

quotes: users can input their accident scenario and get a fault assessment based on Highway Code rules and established precedents

acceptanceCriteria: A scenario selector (e.g. card grid, dropdown, or button group) is present at the top of the tool
Each scenario has a plain-English label and a short description or icon
Selecting a scenario updates the fault matrix, legal references, and case law panels simultaneously
No legal jargon is required to navigate or use the tool

requirements.text: Expand the existing legalReferences.ts and faultMatrix.ts data files to ensure comprehensive coverage of all scenario types used in the selector. Each scenario entry must include: relevant Highway Code rule numbers and summaries, applicable Road Traffic Act 1988 sections, at least one case law precedent with a factual summary and legal principle, and Party A/Party B fault split with rationale.

quotes: based on Highway Code rules and established precedents

acceptanceCriteria: legalReferences.ts contains entries for all scenario types covered by the selector
faultMatrix.ts contains fault split data for all scenario types covered by the selector
Each legal reference entry includes at least one Highway Code rule, one Road Traffic Act section, and one case law entry
Each case law entry includes a case name, factual summary, and legal principle

requirements.text: Apply a consistent, professional visual theme to the fault reference tool page. Use a clean, authoritative legal/insurance aesthetic — dark navy or charcoal tones for headers, amber or gold accents for fault highlights, clear typographic hierarchy, and card-based layouts for the matrix and legal reference panels. The design should feel trustworthy and easy to scan.
acceptanceCriteria: The page uses a consistent colour palette distinct from generic defaults
Headers and section labels have clear typographic hierarchy
Fault matrix and legal reference content is displayed in card or panel containers
Fault percentage highlights use a visually distinct accent colour

constraints: Do not build the bird's eye grid view or the injury photo upload section in this iteration — those are explicitly deferred
All legal reference and fault matrix data must be static (no backend AI inference required for this feature)
Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or any files under frontend/src/components/ui
nonGoals: Bird's eye grid view with AI car placement
AI-driven fault inference from photos or descriptions
Integration with the backend for fault matrix lookups
